scope: compositional re-labeling of 79 body chapter titles only (structural markers excluded)

decision_rules.compositional_definition: title genuinely contains two distinct shape categories where BOTH contribute load-bearing meaning. Strict rule: cross-category, not within-category combination.
single_shape_examples: metaphorical phrases with abstract+concrete elements that fuse into one image (e.g., 'Lavender Eyes', 'Bloody Fields', 'Hot Winds')
place-with-modifier where modifier names the place (e.g., 'The Icy Tomb', 'Tower of Twilight')
named artifacts with conceptual modifier when phrase IS the artifact's proper name (e.g., 'The Clock of Doom', 'Aegis-fang')
abstract+abstract concepts in one phrase (both same bucket; e.g., 'Bonds of Reputation', 'Rights of Victory')
epithets/proper nouns ('City of Sails' = Luskan's epithet, frozen single place-name)
compositional_examples: place+character ('Biggrin's House' — Biggrin is a named character)
object+character ('The Golem's Eyes' — the golem is a creature/character; the eyes are the iconic gems)
character+concept ('The Dragon of Darkness' — Dragon is Shimmergloom, named creature)
place+concept ('Conyberry's Pride', 'The Secret of Keeper's Dale')
quote+character ('If Ever You Loved Catti-brie' — direct address quote with named character)

chapters.title: The Stooge
chapters.notes: Definite descriptor for a single character; single shape.

chapters.title: On the Banks of Maer Dualdon
chapters.notes: Locative phrase naming a specific lake; 'on the banks' is positional, not a second shape.

chapters.title: The Mead Hall
chapters.notes: Specific location; single shape.

chapters.title: The Crystal Shard
chapters.notes: Named artifact; single shape (Crystal modifies Shard, both fuse into one item).

chapters.title: Someday
chapters.notes: Single word read as spoken sentiment.

chapters.title: Bryn Shander
chapters.notes: Bare place name; single shape.

chapters.title: The Coming Storm
chapters.notes: Foreshadowing image; 'Coming' modifies 'Storm' into one image.

chapters.title: Bloody Fields
chapters.notes: Single sensory image; 'Bloody' modifies 'Fields' into one image of carnage.

chapters.title: No More a Boy
chapters.notes: Reads as a character's declaration; single shape.

chapters.title: The Gathering Gloom
chapters.notes: Single sensory image of darkness gathering.

chapters.title: Aegis-fang
chapters.notes: Named warhammer; single artifact name.

chapters.title: The Gift
chapters.notes: Bare definite noun; single shape.

chapters.title: As the Wielder Bids
chapters.notes: Imperative-tone phrase; reads as a character's command.

chapters.title: Lavender Eyes
chapters.notes: Single sensory image (color+body-part fused); not cross-category.

chapters.title: On the Wings of Doom
chapters.notes: Single image — doom personified with wings; the phrase is a unified figure.

chapters.title: Shallow Graves
chapters.notes: Single image of hasty burial.

chapters.title: Vengeance
chapters.notes: Bare abstract noun; single shape.

chapters.title: Biggrin's House
chapters.notes: Biggrin is a named character (frost giant); 'Biggrin's House' is a place defined by character ownership. Both shapes load-bearing — DeepSeek false negative.

chapters.title: Grim Tidings
chapters.notes: Abstract phrase about news/portent; single concept.

chapters.title: A Slave to No Man
chapters.notes: Reads as a declaration of independence; single shape.

chapters.title: The Icy Tomb
chapters.notes: 'Icy' is a descriptive modifier of the tomb; the phrase is a single specific place.

chapters.title: By Blood or by Deed
chapters.notes: Aphorism/spoken phrase; single shape.

chapters.title: Besieged
chapters.notes: Past participle as title; single shape.

chapters.title: Cryshal-Tirith
chapters.notes: Bare place name (Akar Kessell's tower); single shape.

chapters.title: Errtu
chapters.notes: Bare character name (balor); single shape.

chapters.title: Rights of Victory
chapters.notes: Both 'Rights' and 'Victory' are abstract concepts; same bucket — not cross-category compositional.

chapters.title: The Clock of Doom
chapters.notes: Borderline: 'Clock' is artifact, 'Doom' is concept-or-theme. But 'The Clock of Doom' functions as the artifact's proper name (mode of operation = single named artifact, like 'Tower of Twilight' or 'Aegis-fang'). Marking single.

chapters.title: A Lie Within a Lie
chapters.notes: Reads as observed/spoken phrase; single shape.

chapters.title: Other Options
chapters.notes: Abstract phrase for alternatives; single concept.

chapters.title: The Battle of Icewind Dale
chapters.notes: 'Battle' is action-noun head; 'Icewind Dale' is the named place — both load-bearing. Matches DeepSeek.

chapters.title: A Dagger at Their Backs
chapters.notes: Idiomatic image of treachery; single fused image.

chapters.title: City of Sails
chapters.notes: Luskan's epithet; the metaphor of 'Sails' is frozen into the place's identifier — single place-name.

chapters.title: Night Life
chapters.notes: Atmospheric phrase; single image of nocturnal activity.

chapters.title: The Conjuring
chapters.notes: Gerund noun; single action.

chapters.title: The Crags
chapters.notes: Specific geographic feature; single shape.

chapters.title: Sky Ponies
chapters.notes: Sky Ponies is a named barbarian tribe in Salvatore's setting — collective character-name. DeepSeek labeled metaphorical-image; correcting to character-name.

chapters.title: Dagger and Staff
chapters.notes: Two objects in same bucket; single shape (within-category, not cross-category).

chapters.title: To the Peril Of Low-Flying Birds
chapters.notes: Reads as a humorous toast or spoken expression; quote-or-dialogue is the better primary fit. DeepSeek had metaphorical-image.

chapters.title: There Is No Honor
chapters.notes: Declarative line; single shape.

chapters.title: Bonds of Reputation
chapters.notes: User flagged as candidate FN. Strict reading: both 'Bonds' and 'Reputation' are concepts (same bucket). 'Bonds' has metaphorical residue but the title is dominantly an abstract about social standing — single concept-or-theme. NOT compositional under cross-category rule.

chapters.title: Silverymoon
chapters.notes: Bare city name; single shape.

chapters.title: The Trollmoors
chapters.notes: Specific region name; single shape.

chapters.title: The Last Run
chapters.notes: Action-noun; single shape.

chapters.title: Star Light, Star Bright
chapters.notes: Nursery-rhyme quote; reads as borrowed spoken phrase. Refining DeepSeek's metaphorical-image — quote-or-dialogue is the truer fit.

chapters.title: The Golem’s Eyes
chapters.notes: The Guardian's Eyes (rubies/gems) are the iconic object; the Golem (creature/character) provides ownership. Both load-bearing — DeepSeek false negative.

chapters.title: Days of Old
chapters.notes: Abstract reference to nostalgic past; concept-or-theme is the truer fit (DeepSeek had metaphorical-image).

chapters.title: The Challenge
chapters.notes: Action-noun; single shape.

chapters.title: The Secret of Keeper’s Dale
chapters.notes: Keeper's Dale is the place; 'Secret' is a thematic concept — both load-bearing. Matches DeepSeek.

chapters.title: Shadows
chapters.notes: Bare abstract noun; single shape.

chapters.title: End of a Dream
chapters.notes: User flagged as candidate FN. 'End' and 'Dream' are both abstract concepts (same bucket, not cross-category). Re-classifying primary from metaphorical-image to concept-or-theme — the title is a thematic statement, not a sensory image. NOT compositional under strict cross-category rule.

chapters.title: Silver in the Shadows
chapters.notes: Single sensory image of color in darkness.

chapters.title: The Dragon of Darkness
chapters.notes: 'The Dragon of Darkness' = Shimmergloom, the named shadow dragon antagonist. 'Dragon' is character-name (creature); 'Darkness' is concept-or-theme. DeepSeek false negative — labeled as metaphorical-image.

chapters.title: The Broken Helm
chapters.notes: Note: 'The Broken Helm' is also a named inn in Salvatore's lore, which would make it a place-name. Title presents the artifact-name reading as primary; single shape either way.

chapters.title: Eulogy for Mithral Hall
chapters.notes: 'Eulogy' is the abstract concept (mourning tribute); 'Mithral Hall' is the named place. Both load-bearing — DeepSeek false negative (user-flagged).

chapters.title: Tower of Twilight
chapters.notes: Tower of Twilight is Malchor Harpell's named tower — phrase functions as the tower's proper name (cf. 'Aegis-fang', 'The Clock of Doom'). Single place-name.

chapters.title: A Thousand Thousand Little Candles
chapters.notes: Single sensory image; the doubled count is rhetorical not compositional.

chapters.title: Conyberry's Pride
chapters.notes: Conyberry is a named town (place-name); 'Pride' is an abstract concept. Both load-bearing — DeepSeek false negative.

chapters.title: The City of Splendors
chapters.notes: Waterdeep's epithet; frozen into a single place-name (cf. 'City of Sails').

chapters.title: Ashes
chapters.notes: Single evocative image.

chapters.title: Baldur's Gate
chapters.notes: Bare city name; single shape (Baldur is part of the proper name, not a separately invoked character).

chapters.title: Stirrings
chapters.notes: Gerund-derived noun; single shape.

chapters.title: A Plain Brown Wrapper
chapters.notes: Specific physical item; single shape.

chapters.title: Fiery Riddles
chapters.notes: Fused image of fire and puzzle; single shape.

chapters.title: The Weight of a Kings Mantle
chapters.notes: Single figurative image of royal burden; the elements fuse into one metaphor (not cross-category).

chapters.title: Hot Winds
chapters.notes: Single sensory image.

chapters.title: Comrades
chapters.notes: Group epithet for the cast (Companions of the Hall); single character-name.

chapters.title: Paying the Piper
chapters.notes: Gerund idiom for facing consequences; single action.

chapters.title: Dancing Snakes
chapters.notes: Single sensory image; 'dancing' is participial modifier.

chapters.title: The Guide
chapters.notes: Definite descriptor for a single character; single shape.

chapters.title: Never a Fouler Place
chapters.notes: Reads as a character's exclamation about a place; the place is implicit not named — single shape.

chapters.title: Impossible Loyalties
chapters.notes: Abstract phrase; single concept.

chapters.title: Double Talker
chapters.notes: Refers to a specific character (Pook); single shape.

chapters.title: Tricks and Traps
chapters.notes: Pair-image; single bucket.

chapters.title: Black and White
chapters.notes: Single fused color image / moral binary.

chapters.title: Where No Sun Shines
chapters.notes: Single locative image; the place is implied not named.

chapters.title: The Rift
chapters.notes: Geographic feature / Underdark region; single shape.

chapters.title: If Ever You Loved Catti-brie
chapters.notes: Direct address quote that names a specific character (Catti-brie). Both shapes load-bearing — DeepSeek false negative.

chapters.title: Interplanar Goo
chapters.notes: Single whimsical image.

chapters.title: A Walk in the Sun
chapters.notes: Single fused image (action+light).

primary_changes.title: Sky Ponies
primary_changes.rationale: Sky Ponies is a named barbarian tribe in Salvatore's setting

primary_changes.title: To the Peril Of Low-Flying Birds
primary_changes.rationale: Reads as a humorous toast/spoken phrase

primary_changes.title: Days of Old
primary_changes.rationale: Abstract reference to past, not sensory image

primary_changes.title: End of a Dream
primary_changes.rationale: Both elements are abstract concepts; thematic statement not image

primary_changes.title: The Dragon of Darkness
primary_changes.rationale: The Dragon of Darkness is Shimmergloom, Salvatore's named shadow dragon antagonist

compositional_agreement.false_negative_class: DeepSeek consistently missed cross-category combinations involving named characters or places paired with concepts or quotes (place+character, place+concept, character+concept, quote+character). DeepSeek's failure mode appears to be over-classifying names+modifiers as 'metaphorical-image' rather than recognizing two semantically distinct shape categories combining.

directional_verdict_compositional_planni.rate_revised: 10.1% of body chapter titles are compositional (8/79), up from DeepSeek's 2.5% (2/79)
directional_verdict_compositional_planni.previous_verdict: DIVERGE — too low to ship a planner constraint at 2.5%
directional_verdict_compositional_planni.revised_verdict: PASS_PARTIAL — at 10.1%, compositional pairing is a real but minority pattern. Per-book: streams_of_silver 16.7%, halflings_gem 8.0%, crystal_shard 6.7%.
directional_verdict_compositional_planni.ship_recommendation: Encode compositional support as an OPTIONAL feature in the planner's titleShape schema, not a default. The planner schema should allow primary+secondary pair when natural, but ~90% of titles remain single-shape — defaulting to single-shape and offering optional secondary keeps the planner aligned with the corpus distribution.
directional_verdict_compositional_planni.dominant_pair_pattern: place-name + concept-or-theme (3 instances) — abstract-quality + named-place is the most reproducible compositional pattern; appears in all three books except crystal_shard (which uses action+place once).
directional_verdict_compositional_planni.near_miss_pattern: named-character + abstract-quality (2 instances: 'The Dragon of Darkness' = Shimmergloom + Darkness; 'If Ever You Loved Catti-brie' = quote + Catti-brie). Suggests the planner should allow character-name as a viable secondary shape when paired with quotes or concepts.
directional_verdict_compositional_planni.harness_target_update: Update Pattern 26 conclusions entry: compositional rate is 10.1%, not 2.5%. Recommend the planner schema allow optional `secondary_shape` field on `titleShape`, with `concept_or_theme + place_name` as the lead compositional pattern to model in fewshots. Keep the default single-shape distribution from the original DeepSeek pass — those rates remain trustworthy (93.7% primary-shape agreement).
